steps.title: Connect accuracy to equipment
steps.content: The first sentence says Kessler's calculation was more accurate than the 1920 calculation. The next sentence explains why that is unsurprising: the earlier calculation used only cardboard and string.

steps.title: Choose an expected-result transition
steps.content: A signals purpose, C signals similarity, and D signals a final outcome; none introduces an unsurprising explanation. The remaining transition means the following fact is expected given the context. The answer is B.
